meta_title: For Founders: passive income to Solve tutorial hell (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to passive income and overcome tutorial hell. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Passive Income to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Break free from tutorial hell. Learn to build monetizable AI SaaS with this expert-level checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code. Start shipping now.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell is a byproduct of consumption without creation. To escape, pivot from 'learning' to 'shipping' by building micro-SaaS utilities that solve high-pain, low-complexity problems. Leverage automated coding agents like Claude Code to accelerate development, ensuring your MVP reaches market validation before the initial motivation cycle expires.
content.intro:
You’ve watched the videos. You’ve cloned the repos. You’ve mastered the syntax, yet your bank account remains stagnant. You are trapped in the seductive loop of tutorial hell, where the comfort of following instructions replaces the terrifying, necessary act of shipping a product into the wild.

The industry is moving faster than your ability to consume content. The only way to survive the AI gold rush is to stop being a student and start being a founder. By leveraging modern autonomous coding agents, you can compress months of manual development into days of high-leverage execution.

steps.title: Validate the Micro-Pain
steps.description: Identify a repetitive task in a specific niche that costs money or time. Don't build a platform; build a tool that solves one specific problem.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the top 100 complaints in [Niche] subreddits and identify a recurring technical bottleneck that a simple Python script could solve.
steps.claude_code_command: claude research --target 'Reddit/IndieHackers' --query 'what is the most annoying manual task in [Niche]'

steps.title: Architect the Lean MVP
steps.description: Define the minimum feature set required for a paid subscription. Strip away everything that isn't core to the value proposition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a feature breakdown for a [Tool Name] MVP. Prioritize features that allow for a Stripe integration within 48 hours.
steps.claude_code_command: claude architect --focus 'Stripe integration and core utility logic'

steps.title: Scaffold with Autonomous Agents
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate the boilerplate. Focus on clean, maintainable patterns that allow for rapid iteration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust project structure for a FastAPI backend and a minimalist React frontend with authentication.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Scaffold a production-ready FastAPI + React project with Supabase auth'

steps.title: Implement the Revenue Loop
steps.description: Integrate payment processing immediately. If you aren't charging, you aren't building a business; you're building a hobby.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure Stripe webhook handler for subscription billing and user access control based on payment status.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file 'stripe_webhook_handler.py' --logic 'verify signature and update user_subscription table'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Push to a live environment. Don't wait for perfection. Perfection is the enemy of revenue.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a CI/CD pipeline configuration for deploying this application to Vercel and Railway.

pro_tips: Always build with a 'kill switch'—if a feature takes more than 4 hours to implement, it's a candidate for the cutting room floor.
Focus on the 'Last Mile' problem: most SaaS tools fail because they don't integrate with the user's existing workflow.
Use Claude Code to perform 'automated code reviews' on your own PRs to enforce strict type safety and error handling.
Prioritize 'Time-to-First-Dollar' over 'Code Elegance'. You can refactor once you have paying customers.
Monitor user logs via Antigravity to identify which features are actually being used versus which are just bloat.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, shift your focus entirely to distribution. Spend 20% of your time on product updates and 80% on cold outreach, SEO, and building a waitlist.
content.conversion_block: Stop watching others build their dreams while yours sits in a local directory. The market doesn't care about your code quality; it cares about the problem you solve. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
